data.title: Luckin Coffee GAAP EPS of $0.12, revenue of $1.32B
data.description:
• Luckin Coffee press release (OTCPK:LKNCY): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.14
  • GAAP EPS of $0.12.
  • Revenue of $1.32B (+32.7% Y/Y).
  • Average monthly transacting customers were 77.8 million, representing a 24.5% year-over-year increase.
    • Revenues from self-operated stores were RMB7,233.8 million (US$991.2 million), representing a 41.7% year-over-year increase.
    • Same-store sales growth for self-operated stores was negative 3.4%, notably improved from negative 13.1% in the previous quarter, and compared to 13.5% in the same quarter of 2023.

data.title: Bandwidth reports mixed Q4 results; initiates Q1 and raises FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Bandwidth press release (NASDAQ:BAND): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.37 misses by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $209.97M (+27.0% Y/Y) beats by $6.29M.

  • Financial Outlook

    Bandwidth is providing guidance for its first quarter and full year 2025 as follows (in millions) based on current indications for its business.

    For the full year 2025, Bandwidth's revenue guidance projects 8 percent to 11 percent year-over-year growth when adjusting for the expected cyclical reduction in political campaign messaging activity, which resulted in revenue in the first quarter and full year 2024 of approximately $8 million and $62 million, respectively.

    Full Year 2025

    Guidance

    Revenue

    $168 - $170 vs. $182.73M consensus

    $740 - $760 vs. $761.48M consensus

    Adjusted EBITDA

    $16 - $18

    $82 - $90

data.title: Dana Non-GAAP EPS of $0.25, revenue of $2.3B
data.description:
• Dana press release (NYSE:DAN): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.25.
  • Revenue of $2.3B (-8.0% Y/Y).

  • 2025 Financial Targets

    • Sales of $9.525 to $10.025 billion;
    • Adjusted EBITDA of $925 to $1,025 million, an implied adjusted EBITDA margin of approximately 10.0 percent at the midpoint of the range;
    • Operating cash flow of approximately $500 to $600 million;
    • Free cash flow of $175 to $275 million; and
    • Diluted Adjusted EPS of $1.40 to $1.90.

data.title: Berkshire Hathaway further trims DaVita stake
data.description:
• DaVita (NYSE:DVA) shares turned marginally lower on Thursday in the premarket after its major shareholder Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE:BRK.A) (NYSE:BRK.B) further cut its ownership in the dialysis services provider through a second insider sale for the month.
  • According to an SEC filing on Wednesday, the investment conglomerate led by billionaire investor Warren Buffett offloaded 750K shares of DaVita (NYSE:DVA) last Friday at an average price of $154.74 apiece.
  • The transaction worth approximately $116.1M has trimmed Berkshire’s (NYSE:BRK.A) ownership in DaVita (NYSE:DVA) by about 2% to roughly ~35.1M shares, which imply about 44% of outstanding shares of the company’s common stock.
  • It’s Berkshire’s (NYSE:BRK.A) latest insider sale involving DaVita (DVA). A regulatory filing indicated last week that the Omaha, Nebraska-based firm sold approximately 203.1K shares of DVA in a deal worth $31.7M on Feb. 11.

data.title: Walmart beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates Q1 and FY outlook
data.description:
• Walmart press release (NYSE:WMT): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.66 beats by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $180.6B (+4.2% Y/Y) beats by $1.6B.
  • Global eCommerce sales grew 16%, led by store fulfilled pickup & delivery and U.S. marketplace; growth negatively affected by timing of Flipkart’s Big Billion Days sales event.
  • Global advertising business grew 29%, including 24% for Walmart Connect in the U.S.
  • Company provides outlook for Q1 and FY26

data.title: San Francisco 49ers explore 10% stake sale at $9B valuation - report
data.description:
The San Francisco 49ers are weighing a 10% stake sale at a valuation of over $9B, which would make it one of the world's most expensive sports teams, Bloomberg reported, citing people familiar with the matter.

Discussions are ongoing and there's no guarantee that a transaction will be finalized. It's unclear if the team will consider a private equity firm or other investors like wealthy individuals for the deal.

According to CNBC's Official NFL Team Valuations released last September, an NFL team is worth an average of $6.49B. The 49ers were valued at an estimated $7.4B.

The National Football League last year allowed PE players - like Ares Management (NYSE:ARES) and a consortium that includes Blackstone (NYSE:BX) and Carlyle (NASDAQ:CG) - to pick up minority stakes in teams, leading to a flurry of transactions in recent months.

The Miami Dolphins and Buffalo Bills became the NFL's first teams to sell minority stakes to PE firms. Ares (NYSE:ARES) bought a 10% stake in the Dolphins, while Joe Tsai - Alibaba (BABA) co-founder and Brooklyn Nets owner - picked up a 3% stake. The Bills sold a 10% stake to investment firm Arctos Partners.

Jeffrey Lurie, owner of the Super Bowl LIX winner Philadelphia Eagles, eyed selling an 8% stake to private equity players, before finalizing a deal with two family investment groups - which reportedly valued the team at $8.3B.

data.title: Embraer reports early results of offer to purchase certain senior notes due 2028
data.description:
• Embraer S.A. (NYSE:ERJ) Thursday reported the early results of its previously announced offer to purchase for cash up to $150M in aggregate principal amount of the outstanding 6.950% senior unsecured guaranteed notes due 2028.
  • The tender offer was made upon the terms and subject to the conditions set forth in the offer to purchase dated February 5, 2025 relating to the 2028 notes.

data.title: Utz Brands soars after margins expand in Q4
data.description:
Utz Brands (NYSE:UTZ) reported sales fell 3.2% year-over-year in Q4 to $341.0 million. The divestiture of the R.W. Garcia and Good Health brands impacted net sales by -3.2% during the quarter. Organic sales were comparable to last year, led by favorable volume/mix of 0.2% partially offset by lower net price realization of -0.2%. Organic sales increased by 2.9% for the Branded Salty Snacks segment, led by the Power Four Brands, which was offset by an 18.2% decline in Non-Branded & Non-Salty Snacks segment sales, primarily due to declines in Partner Brands and Dips & Salsas.

Adjusted gross profit margin rose 230 basis points during the quarter to 39.4% of sales. The increase was driven by benefits from productivity and favorable sales volume/mix, which more than offset supply chain cost inflation, investments to support the company’s productivity initiatives, and disciplined promotional investments.

Adjusted EBITDA increased 7.5% to $53.1 million. Non-GAAP EPS of $0.22 was reported vs. $0.19 consensus and $0.16 a year ago.

CEO outlook: "Looking ahead to 2025, our strong productivity cost savings driven by our network optimization and increased capital investments gives us the flexibility to build our brands, address consumer value needs, and expand our margins. Moreover, we are accelerating our investments to drive faster share growth in our Expansion geographies while ensuring we hold share in our Core. Importantly, our 2025 outlook positions us well to deliver or exceed our fiscal 2026 bottom-line financial goals."

On the guidance front, Utz Brands (NYSE:UTZ) sees FY25 organic sales growth in the low single-digit and adjusted EPS growth of 10% to 15%.

Shares of Utz Brands (UTZ) shot up 9.8% in premarket action.

data.title: Jumia Technologies AG reports Q4 results; initiates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Jumia Technologies AG press release (NYSE:JMIA): Q4 GMV of $206.1 million, down 12% year-over-year, or up 13% in constant currency.
  • Revenue of $45.69M (-23.1% Y/Y) misses by $3.99M.

  • Adjusted EBITDA loss of $13.7 million compared to $0.6 million in the fourth quarter of 2023.

  • Liquidity position of $133.9 million, a decrease of $30.6 million in the fourth quarter of 2024, compared to a decrease of $26.8 million in the fourth quarter of 2023.
  • Net cash flows used in operating activities of $26.5 million compared to $10.1 million in the fourth quarter of 2023.

  • GUIDANCE

    Jumia remains committed to driving healthy growth, enhancing operational efficiency, and positioning the company for profitability.

    We are currently observing favorable trends in the first quarter, giving us confidence in establishing our full-year 2025 guidance as follows:

    • We anticipate physical goods Orders to grow between 15% and 20% year-over-year.

    • GMV is projected to be between $795 million and $830 million in 2025, a year-over-year increase of 10% and 15%, respectively, excluding foreign exchange impacts.

    • We forecast Loss before Income Tax to be in the range of negative $65 million to negative $70 million, a year-over-year decrease of 33% and 28%, respectively.
